topics.topic: INTRODUCTION TO ADDITIVE MANUFACTURING
Explain the principles, history, and evolution of additive manufacturing
Distinguish between additive, subtractive, and formative manufacturing processes

INTRODUCTION TO ADDITIVE MANUFACTURING Definition and evolution of additive manufacturing (AM), Distinction between AM and traditional (subtractive) manufacturing such as CNC machining, Steps in the AM process and workflow, from design to finished part Advantages, limitations, and scope of AM a cross industries

units.title: MATERIALS FOR AM AND CLASSIFICATION OF AM PROCESSES

topics.topic: MATERIALS FOR AM AND CLASSIFICATION OF AM PROCESSES
Describe the properties and uses of AM materials including polymers, metals, ceramics, and composite materials
Select suitable materials for a given application or process based on mechanical, thermal, and chemical requirements
Identify and describe the seven categories of AM processes as per ISO/ASTM standards
Compare the working principles, materials, advantages, and limitations of various AM technologies like FDM, SLA, SLS, SLM, DED, etc

MATERIALS FOR AM AND CLASSIFICATION OF AM TECHNIQUES Materials for AM: Polymers viz., Thermoplastics (PLA, ABS, Nylon, TPU), thermosets. Metals and Alloys viz., Stainless steel, titanium, aluminium, etc. Ceramics, composites, and functionally graded materials (FGMs). Material properties, selection criteria, and their application in different AM processes. Classification of AM technologies viz., Vat Photopolymerization (e.g., Stereolithography/SLA), Material Jetting, Binder Jetting, Material Extrusion (e.g., Fused Deposition Modelling/FDM), Powder Bed Fusion (e.g., Selective Laser Sintering/SLS; Selective Laser Melting/SLM), Sheet Lamination (e.g., Laminated Object Manufacturing/LOM), Directed Energy Deposition (DED)
Deep dive into MATERIALS FOR AM AND CLASSIFICATION OF AM PROCESSES
Revision
Assessment / Practice / Problem Solving

units.title: PROCESS MODELLING AND ENERGY SOURCES OF AM

topics.topic: PROCESS MODELLING AND ENERGY SOURCES OF AM

Understand how changes in process variables (layer thickness, scan speed, energy intensity, etc.) affect the temperature distribution, residual stresses, microstructure, and overall mechanical properties of printed parts

Explain how different AM technologies use various energy sources (e.g., lasers, electron beams, thermal energy) to achieve material consolidation and how these influence the characteristics and applications of each process
Understand how AM constraints affect product design and development
Identify real-world applications across industries (aerospace, automotive, medical, consumer products, etc.)
Understand the benefits and challenges of AM in industrial settings

PROCESS MODELLING AND ENERGY SOURCES OF AM Process Modelling: Understanding process parameters and their impact (temperature, speed, layer thickness), Path planning and modelling in extrusion -based and photopolymerization systems, Data preparation: CAD to STL conversion, slicing, file handling. Energy sources: Fundamentals of laser, electron beam, and plasma energy sources Material consolidation mechanisms: Sintering, melting, curing, bonding Interaction of energy with material and resultant properties
Revision
Assessment / Practice / Problem Solving
DESIGN FOR ADDITIVE MANUFACTURING (DfAM)
Apply complex design strategies such as topology optimization, lattice structures, and mass customization
Evaluate how AM constraints affect product design and development

DESIGN FOR ADDITIVE MANUFACTURING (DFAM) AND APPLICATIONS Design strategies unique to 3D printing (e.g., lattice structures, topology optimization), Support structures, part orientation, and post-processing requirements. Applications of AM:Industrial sectors: Aerospace, automotive, medical/dental, architecture, art, prototyping, tooling.Emerging research and development areas, Real-world case studies highlighting AM’s benefits and challenges

units.title: POST-PROCESSING AND QUALITY ASSURANCE IN AM

topics.topic: POST-PROCESSING AND QUALITY ASSURANCE IN AM
Identify various post-processing methods: surface finishing, heat treatment, and support removal
Understand the need for quality control, testing, and standardization in AM components

POST-PROCESSING AND QUALITY ASSURANCE IN AM Surface finishing, heat treatment, and other post -build processes, Inspection and testing techniques for AM parts, Standards and quality certification in additive manufacturing. REFERENCES 1.The 3D printing hand book --------- Ben Redwood, Filemon Schoffer, Brian Garret. 2. Additive Manufacturing Technologies --------- Ian Gibson, David W Rosen, Brent Stucker. 3.Anintroductionto3Dprinting ----------------------- Victoria Zukas, Jonas A Zukas
